title: Evangelion 30 Screening at San Francisco Film Festival
display_title: Evangelion 30 Screening at San Francisco Film Festival
wikitext:
[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Wed Jun 19, 2013 7:41 pm Looks like San Fran beat them to the punch. [url]http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/news/2013-06-19/san-francisco-to-screen-evangelio-n-3.0-u.s-premiere[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Wed Jun 19, 2013 8:05 pm $13 for the ticket + $1.70 service fee. The New People theater is not that big, slightly smaller than a regular sized theater where films that have been out for a few weeks are screened although it is 143 seats. Would it be worth it? Last year there was 1.0 and 2.0 double feature, didn't watch it.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Wed Jun 19, 2013 8:05 pm ...No fucking way. I'm going to be in San Francisco on those dates. On a trip that was planned a month ago. My life is awesome.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Wed Jun 19, 2013 8:44 pm AR-99 wrote: $13 for the ticket + $1.70 service fee. The New People theater is not that big, slightly smaller than a regular sized theater where films that have been out for a few weeks are screened although it is 143 seats. Would it be worth it?

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Wed Jun 19, 2013 9:03 pm SF Japantown, where this is going down, is about a 20 min drive for me. Venue info : NEW PEOPLE Cinema is a 143-seat cinema located in the underground level of NEW PEOPLE in San Francisco. Equipped with a cutting-edge HD digital projection and THX®-certified sound system

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Wed Jun 19, 2013 9:30 pm I am incredibly jealous right now, Nuke. Maybe if I find a part time job I can accumulate enough cash to make the trip by then....

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Wed Jun 19, 2013 9:36 pm If it's a 20 minute drive I say go either way. That's not much of an issue & you'd more likely kick yourself in the booty for missing it which would do more damage in the long run. Lol, I thought for you it was like a TRIP that would take X amount of hours. 20 minutes - that's practically next door. Either wayt, it's too early to know how they're going to project it. For the theater this is just some event they have on their schedule after several other events. Hell, the theater themselves may not even know they're playing it yet - that tends to happen on occassion, the theaters are the last to know. But I'd say give them a call or better yet if they have an online customer service email them about a week or so before they run it & ask how they're projected it - disc, harddrive or DCP/CRU? Most theaters worth a damn understand this is an important & pay-or-not question for a lot of film fans & should be able to give you a definite answer. I'd like to assume it's a DCP. That would offer excellent cinema quality & can't be beat. (Yes, I love movies projected on film more but I'd hazard to guess zero film prints were made for 3.0. Let alone a film print with Engligh subtitles). P.S. I wonder.... is it going to be in Japanese or English dubbed? Funimation already owns the rights & it's not like their "quality" dubbing should take any more than two weeks to do.

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Wed Jun 19, 2013 9:53 pm Website says sub. Aren't you checking my links? AFAIK I am the only EGFer in the Bay Area unless someone else hasn't mentioned this. 3.0 is screening 3x - Saturday 7/27 @ 11, Monday 7/29 @ 7, and Sunday 8/4 @ 11. If someone wants to meet up for the screening, PM me. I can't do the 29th so it will likely be the either of the 2 weekend dates, will update when I'm firmed up. If you're driving in I can let you know how to get free parking. ;) Otherwise take Muni to get to Japantown from Powell St. BART. Or you can walk from Civic Center, it's about 15 minutes.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Jun 20, 2013 12:31 pm I remember the 1.0 dub was screened at Sundance Kabuki Cinemas (nearby SF's Kinokuniya). Went on a Monday and we were the only ones in the theater, but it was a fun time anyway. I'd assume 2.0 did a hell of a lot better in theaters than 1.0--and 3.0 will probably top both. Can't wait when the rest of the country gets to see 3.0 on the big screen! Dubs aren't my thing but I'm curious anyway, assuming they'll screen 3.0 with the dub later on.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Thu Jun 20, 2013 12:37 pm Goddamit!! Why never in Mexico!!!???

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu Jun 20, 2013 1:24 pm SEELE-01 wrote: Why never in Mexico!!!???

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Thu Jun 20, 2013 1:46 pm Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: Mexico already has giant bloodthirsty snake gods to worry about, there's no way the Government wants to bring Angels into the mix.

[]:             Quote Post by MarioAyanami128 » Sun Jun 23, 2013 8:10 pm So................ does this make Eva 3.0 eligible for next year's Academy Awards?? Dang it!! Right when I leave the US something like this happens.... >:( Cheers for those who will get a chance to watch this masterpiece on the big screen! ;)

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Sun Jun 23, 2013 9:19 pm MarioAyanami128 wrote: So................ does this make Eva 3.0 eligible for next year's Academy Awards??

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Jun 24, 2013 3:40 pm SEELE-01 wrote: Also, I don't know if they'll screen Evangelion 3.0 or 3.33...

[]:             Quote Post by MarioAyanami128 » Mon Jun 24, 2013 4:54 pm @SEELE-01 Awwwwww............ :( That sucks... But what's the difference between 3.0 and 3.33??? Isn't this the only time that the running time is the same for the theatrical and BD/DVD version? Disconsidering the 7-day requirement, the special rules may eliminate Eva, since I never saw any kind of effort from Funi to promote any of the films seriously. Many of you may call me whatever you want, but I still think that there was something strange about Eva 2.22's absence in the AA that year, when Funi licensed and launched Summer Wars, a film that premiered in Japan later than Eva...

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Jun 24, 2013 5:02 pm MarioAyanami128 wrote: But what's the difference between 3.0 and 3.33???

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Mon Jun 24, 2013 5:51 pm This new, high octane anime sci-fi thriller will be seen for the first time by U.S. audiences exclusively at NEW PEOPLE Cinema on Saturday, July 27th at 11:00am!

[]:             Quote Post by MarioAyanami128 » Mon Jun 24, 2013 7:36 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Mainly animation fixes . IIRC there were adjustments in the audio too. From what I remember, neither 1.0 or 2.0 could be nominated because of Funimation's timing with acquiring both films versus screening them in theaters. There was no conspiracy theory to it.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Mon Jun 24, 2013 9:57 pm If Funimation can get 3.0 to play in one movie theater in one major city for just one week then yes, it would be eligble for the 2014 Oscar ceremony. I doubt it would have a chance to get in - Pixar & Dreamworks literally kill people in order to get their films every year - but if there were enough animation films to qualify for another 5 Film in Best Animation category situation again AND the Academy was feeling very generous for off-the-family-friendly-four-quadrent path then there's a slight chance it could happen. But I'd argue even if all those magical things did come into play it might still be an impossibility since 3.0 is the third part of a four film story. A story that is unseen by practically 95% + of the Academy... if any. Telling Academy voters that they need to watch two other movies & then have the story unresolved with a fourth film still to come is asking too much. If it was Best Picture then sure.... but this is Animation which Hollywood - despite all their biggest blockbusters being practically nothing BUT animation, Avatar is an animated film despite what Cameron says - has always looked down on as something lesser. Either way, who cares. The Oscars are just... bleh. Who won again last year?
